title: Self-Knowledge
whatItIs:
Self-knowledge is the practice of understanding your own patterns: what you value, how you react, what you avoid, and where you tend to become inconsistent or blind.

It draws on psychology, philosophy, reflection, journaling, and feedback from other people. It is less about self-obsession than about reducing the gap between how you think you are and how you actually operate.
whyItMatters:
Self-knowledge matters because people repeat what they do not see. Unexamined habits shape careers, relationships, health, and decision-making long before a person notices the pattern.

The more clearly someone knows their motives and constraints, the better they can choose environments, relationships, and work that fit who they are becoming.
keyIdeas: Patterns matter more than isolated intentions
Journaling and reflection help surface blind spots
frontier: The frontier of self-knowledge includes wearable feedback, journaling tools, mindfulness practice, and better models of identity, habit, and behaviour. The big question is how to help people see themselves more accurately without turning self-optimisation into another trap.
openQuestions: How do you tell the difference between your values and your habits?
Which patterns are stable, and which are just current circumstances?
Can people become more self-aware without becoming self-absorbed?
How much of identity is chosen versus inherited?
